step.title: Run and Observe
step.goal: Execute the prototype (for LOGIC) or present it to the user (for UI), capture observations, and decide whether the question is answered. Iterate at most 2-3 times; beyond that, escalate.

check.action: Proceed to substep 4b for LOGIC execution.
Proceed to substep 4c for UI presentation.

substep.title: Run LOGIC Prototype
substep.action:
Capture stdout, stderr, and the exit code. Store as {{session.run_output}}.
Examples of run_command by language: - Python (PEP 723): cd {{session.prototype_path}} && uv run prototype.py - TypeScript (bun): cd {{session.prototype_path}} && bun prototype.ts - Rust: cd {{session.prototype_path}} && cargo run - Shell: bash {{session.prototype_path}}/prototype.sh

substep.title: Present UI Prototype to User
substep.ask:
UI prototype is ready at: {{session.prototype_path}}/index.html (or per-variant files)
To view: open the file(s) in a browser. Suggested: file://{{session.prototype_path}}/index.html
Once viewed, describe your observations: - What did you see? - Did this answer the question {{session.caller_question}}? - For multi-variant: which variant did you prefer and why?
Or: - Iterate: I need to adjust the prototype (describe what to change) - Cancel: this approach is not working; abort
Capture user's observations as {{session.run_output}}.

substep.title: Evaluate Whether the Question Is Answered
substep.action:
Read {{session.run_output}}. Determine: - Did the prototype produce evidence relevant to {{session.caller_question}}? - Is the evidence factual (observable state) or ambiguous (depends on interpretation)? - Is the question fully answered, partially answered, or unanswered?
Three outcomes: 1. **Answered**: the prototype produced clear evidence. Proceed to step 5. 2. **Partially answered**: more evidence is needed. Iterate (substep 4e). 3. **Unanswered**: the prototype did not work or revealed the question is unprototype-able. Decide whether to iterate or abort.

step.substep:
<check if="iteration is needed AND session.iteration_count < 3">
The first run did not fully answer the question. Iterate?
- Iterate (Recommended if iteration_count < 2): adjust the prototype and re-run - Accept partial findings: record what was learned even though incomplete; move to step 5 - Abort: prototyping isn't working; recommend escalation to a spike ticket
Increment {{session.iteration_count}}. Ask the user (or decide from the run output) what to adjust. Apply the adjustment via Edit to the prototype files. Loop back to substep 4b/4c.
Proceed to step 5; record partial answer.
Return to caller with findings: { status: "aborted", note: "prototyping did not answer this question; consider spike ticket", prototype_path: {{session.prototype_path}} }. ticket-grill will record the abort in its Q&A entry.
Cap reached. Surface to user: "Three iterations did not produce a conclusive answer. This question may not be answerable by prototyping alone. Recommendation: convert to a spike ticket (tag: exploration) for time-boxed investigation." Return to caller with status: "escalate_to_spike".

substep.title: Display Run Summary

section.title: Iteration discipline
section.description: Iterating on a prototype is fine, but capped at 3 attempts. Each iteration that doesn't converge is a signal the question is too broad, the branch was wrong, or the question requires more than a throwaway can give. Beyond 3 attempts, escalate to a spike ticket — that's what spike tickets exist for.

rules.rule: Iteration 1: first attempt. Common to need a small adjustment
Iteration 2: refinement based on run 1's surprises. Acceptable
Iteration 3: last chance. If this doesn't converge, escalate

section.title: UI prototype delivery
section.description: UI prototypes can only be evaluated by the USER looking at them in a browser. The agent cannot screenshot or visually inspect a UI prototype directly. Substep 4c hands off to the user; the agent does NOT pretend to have visual access. If the user describes what they saw, that's the evidence.

section.title: When the prototype crashes
section.description: A crashed LOGIC prototype is informative — it reveals what does NOT work. Capture the stack trace in session.run_output and treat it as evidence. If the crash is in the test code itself (a typo, a missing import), iterate to fix. If the crash is in the underlying behavior being tested, that's the answer to the question.
